label: Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets

node.label: Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets
node.definition: Multi-agent coordination allocates roles, information, incentives, messages, and shared resources among multiple decision-making entities.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets is to connect Observe–plan–act agent loops, Game theory & decision theory to downstream capabilities including Multi-agent systems & organizational intelligence, Game-playing, control & simulation agents, Graph-of-Thought & non-linear deliberation.
examples: substrate for Multi-agent systems & organizational intelligence
substrate for Game-playing, control & simulation agents
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Observe–plan–act agent loops, Game theory & decision theory.

dependencies.rationale: Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets uses Observe–plan–act agent loops as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets uses Distributed systems & networking as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
